page: Amazon Web Services r5n.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance type featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and provides 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, but features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves a strong multi-core CPU performance score of 81307.18 in stress-ng and a PassMark CPU score of 55814.83. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4505.69 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 220.43 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database operations are a key strength, with Redis SET operations reaching 73.84 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring in the top 25%. However, single-threaded performance is average, and LLM text generation speeds are poor, with the medium model generating only 5.9 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5n.24xlarge offers a high-density memory configuration paired with dedicated compute resources and high-speed networking, making it suitable for large-scale memory-bound workloads. The absence of local storage means reliance on network-attached storage, which is supported by its high network bandwidth. This instance is optimized for workloads such as high-throughput database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, large-scale software compilation, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r5n.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 81307.18 and high memory bandwidth of 220.43 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. It excels in database operations, achieving 73.84 million Redis SET operations per second, and software compilation. However, single-threaded tasks and LLM text generation perform poorly. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, in-memory databases, and high-throughput network applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5n.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring ninety-six virtual cores and high-speed networking for demanding database and in-memory caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
Baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps for high-throughput networking
Strong database performance, achieving 73.84 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and large-scale software compilation
